perturbation.original_text: California Business and Professions Code Sections 20000 through 20043 provide rights to you concerning termination or non-renewal of a franchise. If the Master Franchise Agreement contains provisions that are inconsistent with the law, the law will control.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Master Franchise Agreement contains provisions that are inconsistent with the law, the Master Franchise Agreement will control.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the explicit statement that California law provides rights concerning termination/non-renewal and that the law will override the contract if inconsistent. This is a legal contradiction because California law provides specific protections to franchisees during termination and non-renewal, which are being undermined by suggesting the contract will always govern.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Business and Professions Code Sections 20000 through 20043
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code Section 20000 et seq. (The Franchise Relations Act) outlines the rights of franchisees regarding termination and non-renewal, including requirements for good cause and notice. By stating the Master Franchise Agreement will control, it directly contradicts these legal protections provided to franchisees under California law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, BPC 20000 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> Code Search >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Business and Professions Code - BPC DIVISION 8. SPECIAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [18400 - 22949.92.2] ( Division 8 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 44. ) CHAPTER 5.5. Franchise Relations [20000 - 20044] ( Chapter 5.5 added by Stats. 1980, Ch. 1355, Sec. 1. ) ARTICLE 1. Definitions [20000 - 20010] ( Article 1 added by Stats. 1980, Ch. 1355, Sec. 1. ) 20000. This chapter shall be known and may be referred to as the California Franchise Relations Act. (Added by Stats. 1980, Ch. 1355, Sec. 1. Effective October 1, 1980. Operative January 1, 1981, by Sec. 6 of Ch. 1355.)
perturbation.justification: The original text acknowledges California Business and Professions Code Sections 20000 through 20043, recognizing franchisee rights concerning termination/non-renewal, and states the law will control if the Master Franchise Agreement is inconsistent. The changed text removes the explicit mention of franchisee rights and states that the Master Franchise Agreement will control, regardless of inconsistency with the law. This directly contradicts the intention of the California Franchise Relations Act, as described in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that 'This chapter shall be known and may be referred to as the California Franchise Relations Act,' anchoring the legal protection that the changed text is trying to override. The modification creates a situation where the contract dictates terms even if they violate franchisee protections established by California law. Thus, the omission leads to a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You must sign a general release if you renew or transfer your franchise. California Corporation Code 31512 voids a waiver of your rights under the Franchise Investment Law (California Corporations Code 31000 through 31516). Business and Professions Code 20010 voids a waiver of your rights under the Franchise Relations Act (Business and Professions Code 20000 through 20043).
perturbation.changed_text: You must sign a general release if you renew or transfer your franchise.
perturbation.explanation: This change omits the crucial information that California law voids waivers of rights under the Franchise Investment Law and the Franchise Relations Act. Franchisees may not understand their rights are protected by law even when they sign a release.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Corporation Code 31512 and Business and Professions Code 20010
perturbation.law_explanation: California Corporations Code Section 31512 specifically states that any condition, stipulation, or provision purporting to bind any person acquiring any franchise to waive compliance with any provision of the Franchise Investment Law is void. Removing this information deceives franchisees into believing they can waive those rights and introduces a legal risk.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CORP 31512 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Corporations Code - CORP TITLE 4. SECURITIES [25000 - 31528] ( Title 4 added by Stats. 1949, Ch. 384. ) DIVISION 5. FRANCHISE INVESTMENT LAW [31000 - 31528] ( Division 5 added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1400. ) PART 6. GENERAL PROVISIONS [31510 - 31516] ( Part 6 added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1400. ) 31512. Any condition, stipulation or provision purporting to bind any person acquiring any franchise to waive compliance with any provision of this law or any rule or order hereunder is void. (Added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1400.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text omits critical information, creating a legal contradiction. The original text informed the franchisee that waivers of their rights under the Franchise Investment Law and Franchise Relations Act are void. The changed text removes this protection, directly contradicting California Corporations Code Section 31512, which states, 'Any condition, stipulation or provision purporting to bind any person acquiring any franchise to waive compliance with any provision of this law or any rule or order hereunder is void.' By removing this information, the changed text implies that such waivers might be enforceable, undermining the franchisee's rights and creating a situation where they might unknowingly waive protections that the law specifically states cannot be waived.

perturbation.original_text: THE CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE INVESTMENT LAW REQUIRES THAT A COPY OF ALL PROPOSED AGREEMENTS RELATING TO THE SALE OF THE FRANCHISE BE DELIVERED TOGETHER WITH THE DISCLOSURE DOCUMENT.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the statement alerting potential franchisees of their right to have ALL agreements relating to the sale of the franchise delivered WITH the disclosure document. This is a vital protection afforded by the California Franchise Investment Law, and its omission leaves the franchisee unaware of this right. This makes the contract non-compliant because the franchisee can argue that the franchisor failed to deliver all required documents.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Corporations Code Section 31101(c) mandates the delivery of a copy of the franchise agreement, and any related agreements, alongside the disclosure document. By omitting the reminder of this requirement, the franchisor risks non-compliance with this crucial aspect of the California Franchise Investment Law, which aims to ensure franchisees are fully informed before entering into the agreement.

SECURITIES [25000 - 31528] ( Title 4 added by Stats. 1949, Ch. 384. ) DIVISION 5. FRANCHISE INVESTMENT LAW [31000 - 31528] ( Division 5 added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1400. ) PART 2. REGULATION OF THE SALE OF FRANCHISES [31100 - 31158] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1400. ) CHAPTER 1. Exemptions [31100 - 31109.1] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1400. ) 31101. There shall be exempted from the provisions of Chapter 2 (commencing with Section 31110) of this part the offer and sale of a franchise if the franchisor complies with each of the following minimum net worth, experience, disclosure, and notice filing requirements: (a) Net worth. The franchisor and, when necessary, a corporation owning at least 80 percent of the franchisor (parent) meet one of the following net worth requirements, according to financial statements for the fiscal year just ended. The franchisor and the parent, when necessary, may rely upon the immediately preceding fiscal year’s audited financial statement for 15 months from that fiscal year end date. (1) The franchisor has a net worth on a consolidated basis of not less than five million dollars ($5,000,000), according to its audited financial statement. perturbation.justification: The changed text completely omits the requirement for the franchisor to deliver all proposed agreements relating to the sale of the franchise with the disclosure document, a legally mandated consumer protection under the California Franchise Investment Law. This falls under the definition of 'Omission Legal Contradiction' because a legally mandated consumer protection is removed, making it non-compliant. Scraped Law Snippet 1 references California Corporations Code Section 31101, implying regulations around franchise offerings and sales. The original text explicitly reminds the franchisee of their right, whereas the changed text provides no reminder at all, directly contradicting the intent of the law to ensure franchisees are fully informed.
